old_exact: nce that "a line- by-line accounting of the needs of th
new_exact: nce that "a line-by-line accounting of the needs of th
note: The PDF source has 'a line-\nby-line' (hyphenated word split across page lines), which normalizes to 'a line-by-line'. The DB incorrectly captured this as 'a line- by-line' (with unwanted space after hyphen), a clear OCR/ingestion defect. Located in ¶18 (Entzie citation). This is the only substantive content error found; page numbers and form-feeds are documented artifacts per rule 8.

old_exact: [¶23] Olson removed "knowingly" from the jury instructions, changing the offense to a strict liability offense. Olson preserved this issue by arguing the mens rea element should not be removed from the jury instructions. See N.D.R.Ev. 103, Explanatory Note ("The purpose of subdivision (a) is to give the trial court an adequate basis for making a ruling, and to create a record that will permit informed appellate review."); N.B. v. Terwilliger, 2021 ND 74, ¶ 20, 958 N.W.2d 487 (holding appellant did not waive the issue when appellant raised the argument at the hearing and the court subsequently ruled on it).
new_exact: [¶23] Olson contends the district court unconstitutionally removed "knowingly" from the jury instructions, changing the offense to a strict liability offense. Olson preserved this issue by arguing the mens rea element should not be removed from the jury instructions. See N.D.R.Ev. 103, Explanatory Note ("The purpose of subdivision (a) is to give the trial court an adequate basis for making a ruling, and to create a record that will permit informed appellate review."); N.B. v. Terwilliger, 2021 ND 74, ¶ 20, 958 N.W.2d 487 (holding appellant did not waive the issue when appellant raised the argument at the hearing and the court subsequently ruled on it).
note: Critical missing phrase: ¶23 incorrectly begins 'Olson removed' instead of 'Olson contends the district court unconstitutionally removed'. This omission fundamentally misrepresents the appellant's constitutional argument.
OCR artifact from PDF line break: statute number '12.1-02-' wrapped to '02' on next line, creating space-dash-space in DB text '12.1-02- 02'. Should be continuous '12.1-02-02'.

old_exact: order denying for postconviction relief under N.D.R.App.P. 35.1(a)(4). Johnson's application
new_exact: order denying Johnson's application for postconviction relief under N.D.R.App.P. 35.1(a)(4).
note: Paragraph 4 has a missing phrase. The DB text reads 'order denying for postconviction relief' when the authoritative PDF source shows 'order denying Johnson's application for postconviction relief'. The words 'Johnson's application' were apparently dropped during OCR/ingestion, and the sentence structure is corrupted (ending oddly with 'Johnson's application' on next line). The fix restores the complete sentence and corrects the terminating period.
